meta.title: IELTS Speaking Question Bank — Research Report
meta.description: Enhanced with full Part 1 sample questions and Part 2 cue card details (bullets + explain line)

questions: Do you often visit parks?
What do you usually do when you go to a park?
Are there many parks in your city?
Do you think parks are important for a city?
Did you go to parks often when you were a child?
Are you interested in outer space?
Did you learn about space in school?
Have you ever looked at the stars through a telescope?
Do you think space exploration is important?
Would you like to travel to space one day?
Did you enjoy studying science at school?
What area of science interests you the most?
Do you keep up with science news?
How has science changed your daily life?
Do you think children should learn more science?
Do you have a car?
What kind of car would you like to own?
Are cars popular in your country?
Do you think cars will look different in the future?
How often do you travel by car?
How often do you use social media?
Which social media platforms do you use most?
Do you think social media has more advantages or disadvantages?
Has social media changed how you communicate with friends?
Do older people in your country use social media?
Do you wear a watch?
Why do some people prefer expensive watches?
Have you ever received a watch as a gift?
Do you think watches will still be popular in the future?
Is it important to be on time in your culture?
What kind of websites do you visit most often?
Do you have your own website or blog?
How often do you buy things online?
What was the first website you ever visited?
Do you think websites are easier to use on a phone or a computer?
How often do you look at yourself in the mirror?
Do you have mirrors in your home?
Have you ever bought a mirror?
Do you think mirrors are important for decoration?
Would you be comfortable without mirrors for a week?
Have you ever sent handmade gifts to others?
Have you ever received a great gift?
What do you consider when choosing a gift?
Do you think you are good at choosing gifts?
What gift have you received recently?
What kind of things make you happy?
Do you think money can buy happiness?
What do you do when you feel sad?
Is it easy to make you laugh?
Do people in your country express happiness openly?
What is your favourite holiday?
How do you usually spend your holidays?
Do you prefer to travel or stay at home during holidays?
Are holidays important in your culture?
What did you do on your last holiday?
Do you often go to the library?
What kind of books do you borrow from the library?
Are libraries still important in the digital age?
Did you go to the library often as a child?
What facilities does your local library offer?
Do you use headphones often?
What kind of headphones do you prefer?
When do you usually use headphones?
Have you ever bought expensive headphones?
Do you think it's safe to wear headphones in public?
Do you enjoy watching comedy shows?
Are you good at telling jokes?
What kind of jokes do people find funny in your country?
Do you prefer watching comedy alone or with others?
Have you ever been to a live comedy show?
What kind of clothes do you like to wear?
Do you prefer to buy clothes online or in stores?
Has your style changed over the years?
How important is fashion to you?
What do you consider when buying new clothes?
Do you like singing?
Do you sing well?
When do people in your country usually sing?
Have you ever taken singing lessons?
Do you enjoy listening to other people sing?
Have you ever used AI tools like chatbots?
How do you think AI will change daily life?
Are you excited or worried about AI?
Do you think AI will replace some jobs?
Would you trust a decision made by AI?
How concerned are you about your online privacy?
Do you share personal information on social media?
Have you ever been a victim of an online scam?
Do you think companies should collect user data?
What do you do to protect your privacy online?
Have you ever worked or studied from home?
Do you think remote work is more productive?
What are the challenges of working from home?
Would you prefer to work remotely or in an office?
How has remote work changed since the pandemic?
How do you usually communicate with friends online?
Do you prefer texting or video calls?
Has online communication improved your relationships?
Do you think people communicate less face-to-face now?
What apps do you use most for online communication?

part2.topic: Famous Person You Would Like to Meet

cueCard.prompt: Describe a famous person you would like to meet
bullets: How/where you would like to meet him/her
cueCard.explain: And explain why you would like to meet him/her

part2.topic: Person Who Encouraged You to Protect Nature

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who encouraged you to protect the environment
bullets: What he/she encouraged you to do
cueCard.explain: And explain how you feel about this person

part2.topic: Person Who Solved a Problem Smartly

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who solved a problem in a smart way
cueCard.explain: And explain why you think he/she did it in a smart way

part2.topic: Person Who Often Helps Others

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who often helps others
cueCard.explain: And explain how you feel about this person

cueCard.prompt: Describe a friend who learned something new without a teacher

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who makes plans a lot
cueCard.explain: And explain how you feel about this person

part2.topic: Child Who Loves Painting/Drawing

cueCard.explain: And explain why you think he/she loves drawing/painting

part2.topic: Person Who Encouraged You to Achieve Your Goal

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who encouraged you to achieve a goal

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person you are happy to know

cueCard.explain: And explain why you are happy to know this person

part2.topic: Person Who Likes to Talk a Lot

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who likes to talk a lot
cueCard.explain: And explain how you feel about talking to this person

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who thinks music is important
cueCard.explain: And explain why you think he/she feels music is important

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who likes to buy things at low prices

cueCard.explain: And explain why he/she prefers to buy things at low prices

part2.topic: Person with Interesting Ideas/Opinions

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person with interesting ideas or opinions
cueCard.explain: And explain why you find his/her ideas or opinions interesting

part2.topic: Person Who Inspired You

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who inspired you
cueCard.explain: And explain why he/she inspired you

cueCard.prompt: Describe an interesting old person you have met
cueCard.explain: And explain how you feel about this person

cueCard.explain: And explain why you think he/she is successful

part2.topic: Successful Person You Studied/Worked With

cueCard.prompt: Describe a successful person you have studied or worked with
cueCard.explain: And explain how you feel about his/her success

part2.topic: Person You Would Like to Study/Work With

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person you would like to study or work with

part2.topic: Person You Met at a Party

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person you met at a party

cueCard.prompt: Describe a famous person who is a role model for young people
bullets: How young people are influenced by him/her
cueCard.explain: And explain why he/she is a good role model

part2.topic: Person Good at Making People Feel Welcome

cueCard.prompt: Describe a person who is good at making people feel welcome

keyInsights.title: ~60% carry-over rate
keyInsights.detail: About 60% of May-Aug 2026 Part 2 topics are recycled from previous seasons. Only ~37 out of 134 compiled topics appear truly new.

keyInsights.title: Most persistent topics
keyInsights.detail: "Movie You Watched/Enjoyed" and "Sport/Activity You Enjoy" appear in ALL 7 seasons — the most evergreen IELTS cue cards.

keyInsights.title: AI & Digital trend
keyInsights.detail: 2026 introduces AI ("Time You Used AI to Solve a Problem", "Person Who Is a Great Digital Communicator") and digital privacy as new themes.

keyInsights.title: Part 1 most stable
keyInsights.detail: "Art", "Science", "Parks", and "Gifts" appear in every single season (7/7). Safe bets for preparation.

recommendations: Prioritize the Top 20 Part 2 topics — they've appeared in 4–7 out of 7 seasons.
Balance preparation between evergreen topics and new 2026 additions (~28% new).
Build AI & tech vocabulary — new cue cards require domain terminology.
Master the 7 all-season Part 1 topics first — highest predictability.
